data.narrative:
Auterion is a Series B-stage defense and security technology company that has raised $130M to date. Valuation was triangulated against a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms at the same stage, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for Series B value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implied a valuation of $247M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yielded a median valuation of $1.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. The peer set reflects meaningful heterogeneity in defense-tech valuations at this stage.

Triangulation of both methods produced a valuation range of $210M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $724M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression relies on raised capital as a value proxy and may underweight Auterion's operational traction relative to peers, while peer-median anchors to observed market comparables but assumes comparable risk and growth profiles. Key unknowns include revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and the degree to which Auterion's technology differentiation or market position justifies positioning toward the upper or lower quartile of the peer distribution.
